heading: Warrant officers: suspension of laws for promotion or mandatory retirement or separation during war or emergency
label: 12243 Warrant officers: suspension of laws for promotion or mandatory retirement or separation during war or emergency
text: In time of war, or of emergency declared after , by Congress or the President, the President may suspend the operation of any provision of law relating to promotion, or mandatory retirement or separation, of permanent reserve warrant officers of any armed force.May 29, 1954
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)59910:600p (as applicable to reserve warrant officers).34:330g (as applicable to reserve warrant officers).(as applicable to reserve warrant officers), .May 29, 1954, ch. 249, § 1868 Stat. 16534:430d (as applicable to reserve warrant officers).The word “may” is substituted for the words “is authorized, in his discretion”. The words “any provision of law” are substituted for the words “all or any part or parts of the several provisions of law”.
Amendments1994— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 103–337section 599 of this title

Delegation of FunctionsFunctions of the President under this section delegated to the Secretary of Defense, see section 1(4) of Ex. Ord. No. 11390, , 33 F.R. 841, set out as a note under , The President.Jan. 22, 1968section 301 of Title 3
